task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application window is visible on the screen.
The DICOMizer dialog or import tool is open within Weasis.

task_completion.description: Source image is selected for import
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'clinical_photo.jpg' from the Desktop is selected or loaded as the input image in the DICOMizer.
The metadata fields in the DICOMizer show Patient Name as 'Smith^John', Patient ID as 'DERM001', and Modality as 'XC'.

task_completion.description: DICOM file is exported to the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, export confirmation, or file manager shows the resulting DICOM file being saved to or present in '/home/ga/DICOM/exports' (or '~/DICOM/exports').

integrity.description: The agent used the Weasis GUI for conversion
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Weasis graphical interface, rather than terminal commands (like dcmtk/img2dcm) performing the conversion.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Weasis application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window should clearly be Weasis, not a mock application or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not just rename the file extension
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal command simply moving or renaming 'clinical_photo.jpg' to a '.dcm' extension without using Weasis.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source image: /home/ga/Desktop/clinical_photo.jpg. Expected metadata: Patient Name 'Smith^John', Patient ID 'DERM001', Modality 'XC'. Export directory: /home/ga/DICOM/exports.
